cases.scenario: Sharpen the opening of a publish-workflow draft without changing the facts.
cases.draft: I built a Telegram-first publish workflow that stores drafts and post history in MongoDB. It closes the loop from capture to publish and gives me a reviewable audit trail for every step.
cases.feedback: Make the opening sharper and more practical.

cases.scenario: Shorten a prompt-versioning draft while preserving the core argument.
cases.draft: Prompt tuning should happen after a benchmark baseline exists. When prompt versions live in one registry, the team can rerun the same cases and see if the quality, latency, token usage, and cost actually improved.
cases.feedback: Make it shorter and more direct.

cases.scenario: Revise a fallback-focused draft while keeping the OpenRouter lesson intact.
cases.draft: The live smoke proved that plain-text responses still happen. That is not a reason to hide the behavior. It is a reason to track fallback rates, truncation, and cost before changing prompts.
cases.feedback: Make it more practical for builders.
